oakleys-chocolate-blancmange.name: Oakley's Chocolate Blancmange
instructions: Combine chocolate and butter in the top of a saucepan placed over a pot of simmering water; simmer over medium heat until melted, about 5 minutes.

Combine sugar, cornstarch, and salt in a bowl; stir in about 1/4 cup milk. Add mixture to melted chocolate and blend thoroughly. Add remaining milk. Place saucepan over direct heat and cook, stirring constantly, until mixture boils and thickens, about 1 minute more. Return the saucepan to the pot of boiling water, cover, and cook 15 minutes longer, stirring occasionally.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in vanilla. Cool in the refrigerator, about 3 hours to overnight before serving.

octopus-meatballs-argiro.name: Octopus Meatballs
instructions: Place the octopus in a pot first. Cover it with water and let it simmer until it softens. Depending on the octopus, from 40 minutes up until it softens well.
Drain (we keep its juice) and after it cools the mince or grind it in the blender. Place in a bowl.
Soak the bread in the dough that boiled the octopus. Drain it well and rub it in crumbs in the octopus minced meat.
Add all the other ingredients and salt carefully, because the fresh octopus has enough salt. We ferment all the ingredients. If the dough is thin, add some toast.
Create small octopuses, flour them, shake them and brown them in hot olive oil.
We drain them into absorbent paper.

octopus-with-spicy-spaghetti-argiro.name: Octopus With Spicy Spaghetti
instructions: Clean the octopus (turn its hood and remove its interior, also press and remove its mouth from the center).
Wash the octopus well and cut it with a knife in large bites.
In a medium pot, add the oil and onions.
Sauté for 5 minutes, stringing until the opinions are polarized and transparent.
Place the octopus pieces in the pot and let them cook over medium heat.
At this stage it will take a nice red dark color and enhance its taste.
Sprinkle with the spices and mix to remove their perfume.
Add the tomato paste, the laurel and rub in the pot, cook and smell.
Add the ingredients with the wine and degrave (grab) the base of the pot.
After the alcohol evaporates for a few minutes, add the ground tomatoes, as well as enough water, so that it covers our ingredients.
Allow the food covered to simmer until the octopus softens well. About 35 to 40 minutes.
Add the cut pasta and if necessary, some extra water (to boil the pasta).
Simmer on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring regularly until the cut macaroni boils and the food stays with a little zoom.

Check if it needs salt and rub pepper. Especially if your octopus is fresh, you may not have to salt. It's pretty salty sometimes. Instead, you may need to add a pinch of sugar to balance the flavors).
Remove the pot from the heat while the food is still juicy and add two ice cubes to drop the temperature and stop boiling the pasta.
Serve while it is still warm and juicy. If we want, sprinkle with some parsley.

old-fashioned-giblet-stuffing.name: Old-fashioned Giblet Stuffing
instructions: Chop giblets into small pieces.

Melt butter in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add giblets and cook for 2 minutes. Add celery and onion; cook and stir until tender, about 5 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in poultry seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Place bread crumbs into a large bowl. Stir in giblet mixture and enough broth to lightly moisten bread. Stuffing is ready for baking as desired.

one-pan-herbed-chicken-and-veggies.name: One-pan Herbed Chicken And Veggies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C).
Place the asparagus and cherry tomatoes on opposite sides of a large, rimmed baking sheet.
Place the chicken breasts in the middle section of the baking sheet.
Drizzle the veggies and chicken with the oil.
Sprinkle on the salt, pepper, basil, thyme, and rosemary, ensuring all the ingredients are evenly coated in the oil and seasonings.
Bake for 25 minutes or until chicken’s internal temperature reaches 165˚F (72˚C).
Allow the chicken to rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Place the chicken breast on a plate with portions of the roasted veggies and serve.
Enjoy!

one-pot-chicken-enchilada-mac-and-cheese.name: One-pot Chicken Enchilada Mac And Cheese
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook elbow macaroni in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 8 minutes. Drain and set aside.

Combine enchilada sauce, half-and-half, and green chiles in the same pot over medium heat and cook until warm, 5 to 10 minutes. Slowly add Cheddar cheese and pepperjack cheese and whisk continuously until melted, about 5 minutes. Return cooked macaroni to the pot. Add chicken and cilantro and stir until combined and heated through, about 5 minutes more.

onion-beef-gravy.name: Onion Beef Gravy
instructions: Spray a saucepan with cooking spray. Add garlic and cook over medium heat just until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add water, onion soup mix, and beef base; bring to a boil. Cover the pan and simmer until onions are tender, about 20 minutes.
Combine milk and flour in a jar with a lid; shake until combined and whisk into the saucepan. Bring gravy back to a boil and cook until thickened, about 10 minutes. Season with black pepper.

opium-twist-recipe-lezzet.name: Opium Twist Recipe

ingredients.item: Instead of traditional flour, you can use full wheat flour or gluten.
Instead of grape vinegar, you can choose apple vinegar, lemon juice or white vinegar.
You can make the dough softer and aromatic using warm s&uuml instead of water.
Liquid oil can be preferred instead of melted butter or olive oil.
Walnut, nuts or sesame can also be used instead of crushed poppy.
You can add a more aromatic taste interior a small amount of cinnamon or sugar.
Instead of green lentilization, nohut porridge or beef barbunya can be used.
Instead of Maydanoz, you can add aroma &ccedil with dereotu, fresh mint or roka.
Spices can adjust to your taste; can increase the quantity of cumin, pepper or pepper.
Instead of sesame or pepper, precipitation sprinkle buckthorn or flax seeds.

2. A soft dough kneading, which does not stick to the water by adding the azar.
3. Wrap the dough with stretch and rest for 20-30 minutes.
4. Take a bowl of boiled lentils, add chopped parsley and spices.

orange-cumin-vinaigrette.name: Orange Cumin Vinaigrette
instructions: Place olive oil, rice wine vinegar, orange zest, orange juice, Dijon mustard, cumin, and red pepper flakes into a jar with a screw top; season with salt and black pepper.
Cover jar; shake vigorously to form a temporary emulsion, about 2 minutes.

orange-pie-without-leaf-argiro.name: Orange Pie Without Leaf
instructions: We grow a long baking pan of 30×20cm or a round of 30 million.
First, we'll make the syrup to cool down.

osso-buco-recipe-with-mashed-potatoes-co.name: Osso Buco Recipe With Mashed Potatoes
osso-buco-recipe-with-mashed-potatoes-co.original_name: Receta de osobuco con puré de papas

ingredients.item: 4  piezas de osobuco de ternera de 4-5 cm de grosor ((aprox. 250 g cada una))
2 cdas de harina de trigo (para sellar la carne)
2 medium carrots, cut into small cubes
400 gr de tomate triturado (puede ser enlatado de buena calidad)
800 gr de papas  ((idealmente variedad harinosa como la yukon gold o papa pastusa))
80 ml de crema de leche  ((nata para cocinar))
Sal y nuez moscada al gusto

instructions: It well secates the pieces of osocouple and pits them slightly by flour, shaking the excess.
In a big pot or deep frying pan, heat up olive oil and seal the flesh from both sides to golden. Withdraw and reserve.
In the same pot, fry onion, garlic, celery and carrot for 10 minutes with medium heat.
Add white wine and reduce alcohol for 3 minutes.
Add crushed tomato, aromatic herbs, salt and pepper to your taste. Reintegrate the osocouple and cover with the broth.
Fully low-heat kitchen for 2 hours or till meat is so tender as to get rid of with a fork. It can also be made in an oven at 160 been 3 been during the same time.

our-mashed-potatoes-ever.name: Our Mashed Potatoes Ever

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 2 0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
A potato ricer or food mill fitted with fine disk

instructions: Place 4 lb. medium Yukon Gold potatoes, scrubbed, in a large pot and pour in cold water to cover by 1". Add a large handful of kosher salt (water should taste briny, like the ocean) and bring to a boil on the stovetop over high heat. Reduce heat and simmer until potatoes are fork-tender (very tender but not crumbly, 30–35 minutes). Drain, briefly rinse with cool water to remove any excess starch, and return potatoes to warm pot (off heat) to dry while you heat the milk mixture.
Warm 1½ cups whole milk, ½ cup heavy cream, 1 head of garlic, halved crosswise, and 3 sprigs rosemary in a small saucepan over medium until fragrant, about 5 minutes. Remove pan from heat.

Pass hot potatoes through ricer or food mill into a large bowl (cold potatoes will become gummy; ricer will catch potato skins, remove and discard as needed if they start to block grate). Add 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature, cut into pieces, and 4 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 2½ tsp. Morton kosher salt and stir until butter is completely incorporated. Strain warm milk mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large measuring glass. Pour into potatoes ½-cupful at a time, stirring after each addition until liquid is fully incorporated and mixture is smooth before adding more.

Serve mashed potatoes topped with a few grinds of freshly ground black pepper.
Do ahead: Mashed potatoes can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Reheat over medium, stirring often and adding ¼ cup milk or stock to thin out if needed.
Editor’s note: This mashed potato recipe was first printed in our November 2017 issue as “Ultra-Creamy Mashed Potatoes;” it has been updated for style. Head this way for more of our favorite potato recipes →

oxtail-bourguignonne.name: Oxtail Bourguignonne
instructions: Cook bacon in heavy large pot over medium-high heat until brown and crisp. Using slotted spoon, transfer bacon to plate. Pour drippings into small bowl. Return 6 tablespoons drippings to pot (add olive oil, if necessary, to measure 6 tablespoons total; reserve bacon for another use). Tie parsley, thyme, and bay leaves together for bouquet garni. Stir 1 tablespoon flour and butter in small bowl to smooth paste.
Whisk 1 1/2 cups flour, 2 teaspoons salt, 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, and nutmeg in medium bowl. Add oxtails, a few pieces at a time, to seasoned flour and toss to coat.
Heat bacon drippings in pot over medium-high heat. Working in batches, add oxtails and brown on all sides, about 6 minutes per batch. Transfer oxtails to bowl after each batch.

Reduce heat to medium-low. Add chopped onions, diced carrot, and minced garlic to pot. Sauté until onions soften, 5 to 6 minutes. Return oxtails and any accumulated juices to pot. Add bouquet garni, then broth and wine. Bring to boil. Cover and simmer until meat is almost tender, adjusting heat occasionally to maintain gentle simmer, about 3 hours. Mix in mushrooms, shallots, carrot chunks, and whole garlic cloves. Increase heat and return to boil. Reduce heat to low. Cover pot and simmer gently until meat and vegetables are tender, about 45 minutes longer.

Tilt pot and spoon off any fat that rises to surface. Stir flour paste into stew. Simmer uncovered until sauce thickens slightly, stirring occasionally, 6 to 8 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Cool 1 hour. Refrigerate uncovered until cold, then cover and keep refrigerated. Rewarm over low heat before serving.
